And really what they're trying to figure out when they are sitting across the desk or sitting across the table on one level on the side kind of the marketing level They're trying to figure out can I trust this person? Will they do what they say they're going to do if I buy this thing will it work if it doesn't will they make it right? Will it take six tries to get it right or are they going to do it right the first time? I mean that's sort of at the at the marketing level the the very the very Conscious thinking that's going on by the prospect the customer or the client and it really doesn't matter whether they're Like picking a restaurant something really simple So I mean like you know the reason Domino's worked when it worked is because What people wanted to know when they ordered a pizza? Was was the damn when was the damn thing going to get there and was it still go be hot, right? And so on a very simplistic level that was the marketing issue and it's such a simplistic and commoditized product That's really all that had to be answered People aren't going to buy a pizza or not buy a pizza based on Their philosophical connection to Tom Monahan, right? Now they might buy a franchise or not buy a franchise based on their philosophical connection to Tom Monahan, but not the pizza unless Now they might not buy a pizza based on what they come to know about the philosophy of the guy leading the company That's quite possible, right? But generally speaking. It's not part of the transaction But in more complex buying just decisions There's this one level going on of will it work like they say it will work and will they deliver it And can I trust the guy or will he rip me off if he tells me he's gonna? Fix the porch and it's only going to be a one-day job. Will it be a one-day job or will it be a two-week job? I mean that's what's going on on the marketing level, right on another level and I think the more The more money that is involved Or the more other commitment that is involved whether it's a commitment of time or a Risk of face by something not working out or whatever Then on another level. I think that what? Really people are trying to figure out that they might not it's clearly enunciate as they would the other things They're trying to figure out is are Are we in sync do we have some shared understanding some shared values some shared philosophy and Again at the simple commodity level iced tea in a jar which hotel you stay and you know This is largely not in play, but more complex sales More More fee more price more commitment. I think it's very much in play And by the way, I think it's why like at a certain point in deal-making and or in selling I think I Think there is still no substitute for face-to-face because That's really what's going on in a face-to-face. There's one conversation going on About the deal There's like another conversation going on about can I trust this person in this and then there's like a conversation not going on But that really is going on about what is this person? What is this person? What is this person really about? you know and not what do they say but What are they like and we're trying to you know and you'll Many guys your wives will somebody said to me the other day Well, we know we went to lunch with them because they had to pass my wife's smell test Right, and of course they didn't literally mean You know they meant Intuition I guess is what they meant, but that's really the wrong word for it It's just I think by the way largely speaking that women have better radar about Some things with people then then guys do but but I think everybody's sort of trying to figure this out and and and there's certainly more count more comfortable Doing business with doing deals with having monetary relationships with somebody with whom there is Philosophical values agreement then there is Non-agreement and certainly more comfortable than there is Disagreement I mean
